notes: Each case used one published pathology-bearing key-image link from the public teaching page.
The selected auto-scroll link index was pre-recorded per case because not every page used the first link for the target pathology.

This corrected aggregate fixes an earlier ad-hoc scoring bug in the pulmonary embolism case. The final regex requires pulmonary-embolism terms and no longer overcounts unrelated diagnoses containing the letters pe.

scoring_rules.match: Diagnosis text contains the target concept.
scoring_rules.partial: Diagnosis captures a clinically related but incomplete target, such as appendicitis instead of perforated appendicitis.
scoring_rules.miss: Diagnosis points to a different pathology.

prompt_template: You are evaluating a single de-identified DICOM teaching-case slice for research only, not clinical care. Return only the structured schema. Use the attached image as primary evidence and the metadata only as supporting context. Be specific about the single most likely diagnosis visible on this slice.

cases.title: CASE 1: Acute appendicitis
visible_findings: Symmetric triangular areas of dense sclerosis along the iliac side of both sacroiliac joints, greatest inferiorly
Sacroiliac joint spaces are preserved
No clear erosions, joint-space widening, or ankylosis are visible on this slice
The sclerosis is predominantly iliac-sided rather than involving both sides of the joints equally
cases.reasoning: The dominant abnormality on this slice is bilateral, sharply marginated triangular iliac-sided subchondral sclerosis adjacent to the sacroiliac joints with preserved joint spaces and no obvious erosive change. That pattern is most characteristic of osteitis condensans ilii and is less typical of inflammatory sacroiliitis.
uncertainties: This is a single axial slice, so full extent and chronicity cannot be assessed
Subtle erosive or inflammatory changes elsewhere in the sacroiliac joints could be missed on this image alone, but the appearance here strongly favors osteitis condensans ilii

cases.title: Case 14: Acute pulmonary embolism
cases.selected_key_text: segmental left upper lobe pulmonary artery embolus
cases.diagnosis: Thoracic aortic dissection, most likely involving the ascending aorta/aortic arch (likely Stanford type A)
visible_findings: Abnormal contrast-filled thoracic aorta with apparent enlargement of the ascending/arch segment
Curvilinear low-attenuation line within the aortic lumen, suggesting an intimal flap
Appearance of separated luminal compartments within the aortic contour, favoring true and false lumina rather than a single normal lumen
cases.reasoning: The dominant abnormality on this slice is centered in the thoracic aorta rather than the lungs or pleural spaces. The combination of an enlarged aortic contour and a suspected intraluminal flap/double-lumen appearance is most consistent with aortic dissection on this image.
uncertainties: This is a single slice, so the full extent of disease and exact classification cannot be confirmed from this image alone.
Ascending aortic motion or contrast-mixing artifact can mimic a flap on CT and lowers confidence.
Review of adjacent CTA slices would be needed to confirm the diagnosis and distinguish it from aneurysmal dilatation without definite dissection.

cases.title: Perforated sigmoid diverticulitis
cases.selected_key_text: locules of free air adjacent to sigmoid colon
cases.diagnosis: Gas-containing pelvic abscess, most likely secondary to perforated sigmoid diverticulitis
visible_findings: Irregular rim-enhancing fluid collection in the central pelvis posterior to the urinary bladder
Multiple internal gas locules within the collection
Adjacent thickened inflamed bowel wall in the rectosigmoid region
Surrounding pelvic fat stranding/inflammatory change
cases.reasoning: The dominant abnormality on this slice is a thick-walled gas-containing fluid collection in the pelvis with adjacent inflamed rectosigmoid bowel and surrounding stranding, which most strongly favors a pelvic abscess from complicated/perforated sigmoid diverticulitis.
uncertainties: A single axial slice is insufficient to confirm the exact organ of origin of the collection.
Less likely alternatives on this slice include a tubo-ovarian abscess or infected fluid-filled uterus if the central pelvic structure is gynecologic rather than colonic.

cases.title: CT Abdomen and Pelvis - Cecal diverticulitis
cases.selected_key_text: inflammatory stranding adjacent to cecal diverticulum
visible_findings: Symmetric triangular subchondral sclerosis is present along the iliac side of both sacroiliac joints
The sacroiliac joint spaces appear preserved on this slice
There is no clear erosive change or ankylosis visible at the sacroiliac joints on this image
cases.reasoning: The dominant visible abnormality is bilateral, symmetric iliac-sided sacroiliac sclerosis with preserved joint spaces, which is most characteristic of osteitis condensans ilii rather than inflammatory sacroiliitis.
uncertainties: Assessment is limited by having only a single axial slice
Subtle erosions or additional abnormalities elsewhere in the sacroiliac joints cannot be excluded without the full study
If clinically relevant bowel or pelvic soft-tissue pathology exists elsewhere in the exam, it may not be represented on this image

cases.title: Sigmoid diverticulitis
cases.diagnosis: Small-bowel neuroendocrine tumor (carcinoid) with calcified mesenteric metastasis and desmoplastic reaction
visible_findings: Stellate/spiculated soft-tissue abnormality in the central mesentery
Dense central calcific focus within that mesenteric lesion
Radiating fibrotic-appearing strands and mesenteric tethering around the lesion
Crowding/retraction of adjacent small-bowel loops in the involved mesentery
cases.reasoning: The combination of a calcified mesenteric mass and surrounding desmoplastic-appearing mesenteric reaction is most characteristic of metastatic small-bowel carcinoid/neuroendocrine tumor on CT.
uncertainties: This is a single coronal slice, so the primary bowel lesion is not confidently identified on this image alone.
The mesenteric abnormality could overlap in appearance with less likely entities such as sclerosing mesenteritis or other calcified mesenteric masses.

cases.title: Strangulated hernia with small bowel ischemia
cases.expected_diagnosis: hernia complicated by small bowel ischemia (strangulation)
cases.selected_key_text: hypoenhancing small bowel loops in the hernia sac
cases.diagnosis: Strangulated ventral abdominal wall hernia causing closed-loop small-bowel obstruction, with probable ischemic change
visible_findings: Large anterior ventral abdominal wall hernia containing multiple bowel loops
Clustered, dilated fluid/gas-filled small-bowel loops within the hernia sac
Crowding/twisting of mesenteric vessels and marked mesenteric fat stranding at the hernia neck
Abnormal bowel wall appearance in the herniated loops with edema/thickening, raising concern for compromised perfusion
cases.reasoning: The dominant abnormality on this axial venous-phase CT slice is a bowel-containing ventral hernia with obstructed-appearing clustered small-bowel loops and congested twisted mesentery at the neck. That combination is most consistent with incarceration/strangulation rather than a simple reducible hernia, and the edematous bowel/mesentery makes ischemic compromise the best single-slice diagnosis.
uncertainties: This is a single slice, so the full transition point and extent of obstruction cannot be confirmed.
Definite bowel ischemia is difficult to prove on one image alone because mural enhancement, pneumatosis, and associated free fluid require review of adjacent slices.

cases.title: Perforated appendicitis
cases.selected_key_text: mucosal discontinuity with extraluminal air and fluid
cases.diagnosis: Acute appendicitis, likely retrocecal in the right lower quadrant
visible_findings: Focal inflammatory change in the right lower quadrant centered near the cecal base
Enlarged thick-walled tubular/oval soft-tissue structure just anterior to the right psoas muscle, in the expected appendiceal region
Adjacent periappendiceal/pericecal fat stranding
Mild reactive thickening of nearby cecal/ileocecal bowel wall
cases.reasoning: On this axial contrast-enhanced CT slice, the main abnormality is a focal inflamed tubular structure adjacent to the cecum in the right lower quadrant with surrounding fat stranding. That distribution and morphology are most typical of acute appendicitis, particularly a retrocecal appendix.
uncertainties: This is a single slice, so the full blind-ending course of the appendix cannot be confirmed with certainty.
A focal cecal diverticulitis or terminal ileal inflammation is less likely but cannot be fully excluded from this slice alone.
The slice is insufficient to determine whether there is perforation, abscess, or an appendicolith elsewhere in the series.

cases.title: Appendicitis in pregnancy
cases.selected_key_text: appendiceal lumen distended to >6 mm with T2-hyperintense contents
cases.diagnosis: Acute appendicitis in pregnancy, likely involving a superiorly displaced right-sided appendix
visible_findings: Gravid uterus is present, with bright amniotic fluid and visible fetal parts occupying much of the abdomen/pelvis on this axial T2 image
Along the maternal right lateral abdomen there is a probable enlarged tubular structure with T2-bright luminal fluid and a thickened wall, in the expected region of a pregnancy-displaced appendix
Mild surrounding T2-bright periappendiceal inflammatory change/fluid is visible adjacent to that structure
The enlarged uterus displaces adjacent bowel/cecal structures superiorly and laterally, which fits the expected altered appendiceal position in pregnancy
cases.reasoning: The dominant abnormality on this slice is a probable inflamed fluid-filled tubular structure along the maternal right side with adjacent edema, while the uterus is clearly gravid. In pregnancy the appendix is often displaced cephalad and laterally by the enlarged uterus, and this single-slice appearance most strongly favors acute appendicitis in pregnancy over other right-sided bowel or adnexal structures.
uncertainties: This is only one axial slice, so the appendix cannot be followed as a definite blind-ending structure with certainty
Alternative mimics such as a small inflamed bowel loop or adnexal structure cannot be completely excluded on this image alone
This slice is insufficient to determine whether there is perforation, abscess, or the full extent of surrounding inflammation

cases.title: Acute left MCA infarction
cases.expected_diagnosis: acute left multifocal MCA distribution ischemia/infarction
cases.selected_key_text: patchy left multifocal MCA distribution diffusion restriction
cases.diagnosis: Sporadic Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (CJD) with cortical ribboning
visible_findings: Patchy bilateral gyriform hyperintensity along the cortical ribbon at the high frontoparietal convexities on this axial diffusion-weighted image
Predominantly cortical involvement with relative sparing of the underlying deep white matter
Multifocal bilateral distribution that does not fit a single vascular territory
No obvious mass effect or hemorrhagic focus visible on this slice
cases.reasoning: The dominant abnormality is bilateral cortical diffusion hyperintensity producing a cortical-ribboning pattern near the vertex. On diffusion-weighted brain MRI, this appearance is most suggestive of sporadic CJD and is less typical for a focal infarct.
uncertainties: This is only one superior slice; ADC correlation and review of the full exam are needed to confirm true restricted diffusion and assess additional characteristic involvement.
Similar cortical diffusion hyperintensity can be seen with postictal change, hypoxic-ischemic injury, or encephalitis.
If the signal on the full series is centered more at the corticomedullary junction than in the cortex itself, neuronal intranuclear inclusion disease would be an important alternative.

cases.title: Cauda equina compression from severe stenosis
cases.expected_diagnosis: severe central stenosis with compression of the cauda equina at multiple levels
cases.selected_key_text: severe L3-4 and L4-5 central stenosis with compression of the cauda equina
cases.diagnosis: Bilateral lumbar juxtafacet (synovial) cysts causing severe central spinal canal stenosis
visible_findings: Two symmetric T2-hyperintense rounded cystic lesions are present in the posterolateral spinal canal adjacent to the facet joints.
Marked compression and near-effacement of the thecal sac/CSF space is seen, indicating severe central canal narrowing.
Associated degenerative posterior element/facet hypertrophy is visible around the cystic lesions.
There may be mild accompanying broad-based posterior disc bulging, but the dominant canal compromise on this slice is posterior and juxtafacet.
cases.reasoning: On this axial T2 lumbar MR slice, the dominant abnormality is a pair of bright cystic-appearing masses centered at the facet joints and projecting medially into the canal. Their juxtafacet location, fluid signal, and mass effect on the thecal sac are most consistent with bilateral synovial cysts, producing severe degenerative lumbar spinal stenosis.
uncertainties: This is a single axial slice, so exact level, full extent, and continuity with the facet joints cannot be definitively confirmed.
A close differential on one slice is marked facet joint effusions or another cystic degenerative posterior epidural lesion, but bilateral juxtafacet synovial cysts are the best fit here.
Assessment of additional contributors such as spondylolisthesis or multilevel stenosis requires the rest of the exam.
